reviews.review_description:
Great "The Settlers" style sim of terraforming Mars.

No replayability, but had me thoroughly engrossed for 4 evenings, so good value. Well done Devs.

It’s not often a game captures my attention enough for me to see it through to completion, but this game did. It would be nice to see more in depth combat and factory mechanics. The ending was a bit anticlimactic once my colony was fully set up there wasn’t anything to do but wait for it to finish and I expected something to happen once I’d unlocked the whole planet. However, watching Mars slowly transform while you play is awesome and I got a ton of enjoyment out of the game leading up to the finale. Highly recommend. Hopefully we get a sequel in the future.

reviews.review_title: The best strategy game in quest so far
reviews.review_description:
The mechanics and graphics plus the pacing on hope fast everything moves along
Tough learning curve in some mechanics to keep me on my toes (I love a challenge)
Literally an ai terraforming mars what a cool concept idea
The movement feels good
I’ve really enjoyed this game
Thanks!


If this had multiplayer it would be on par with civilization
Maybe some more customization to your combat units before that happens
I hope you make another with multiplayer 🤞

the only downside was replayability but definitely a fun few days
Graphics could be better, since the quest 3 has so much more potential. Seems like resolution is scaled down. Giving it 5 stars for the gameplay, since that's what matters for this kinda genre.

reviews.review_title: How do you upgrade the roads?
reviews.review_description:
I remember loving this game on my PC so I wanted to try it in vr form and thus far I am enjoying the new perspective. My one complaint is that it does not seem possible to improve the roads once the appropriate technology has been developed. Granted I might be doing something wrong but I would appreciate some instruction on the issue.

A few more minutes of gameplay and I have learned that the roads upgrade automatically in the vr version.

reviews.review_title: Great game but needs some tweaks
reviews.review_description:
Developers great work lovely story line, loved it to a point, but I became overwhelmed and frustrated… quickly.

I keep feeling overwhelmed by the size and having to micromanage everything directly eg upgrades etc . Simple fix would be to have a tablet with overview where you can look at a list of all buildings by type or current upgrades and upgrade them in bulk or direct from that list click on it to go there. I got to stage 3/4 before it was to much to comprehend and keep a nice fun flow in the game without out a grind. The above is quite standard in these types of games…
Save system is broken, I cannot access my saves, nore load up a saved game makes me start a fresh game whenever I I try to load into a saved game

Wonderful game! read the reviews. This is not a review, but a message for developer. 

My only gripe, I feel this would be better experienced without controllers.

Needs 100% hand tracking. Then we’ll be on a whole different realm of immersion & physical comfort. 

5 stars when we get the ability to play this game entirely with our hands.

developer_response.body:
Hello there!

Thanks a lot for taking the time to give us a review, even if you say it is not hahaha

Hand tracking was sadly out of our scope when making the game and currently we have no announcements regarding any game so that 5th star may never come u.u

But 4 stars is more than enough to make us happy, hearing people say and write about how they enjoy our games is one of the best parts of our job so thank you very much

Ad Astra!

reviews.review_description:
Had high hope for this one.  I love simcities and such but this one.  Just Bof

I played like 10=20 hours.  Got the Terraformer 4 award and the immortal award and many more.  But i still don't know what an AquaDome really does.

The voice acting is great specially from Amy.  The story is nice but does it have an ending ?   I need to bring down the planet temperature but how ?  Or is that it when you beat Carmine ?    

Yep just Bof

developer_response.body:
Hello!

Thank you so much for taking the time to write us a review, we appreciate all kinds of feedback :3

Ad Astra!

reviews.review_description: Just getting into it and it is difficult to establish routines. This game could definitely use a comprehensive tutorial.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review!

We appreciate all the feedback we can get and we hope that you will keep playing our game in the future :3

Ad Astra!

reviews.review_description: The game was difficult in the beginning for me because, I don't normally play these type of games. I prefer FPS, but bought this on discount, even with the tutorial mode the game doesn't baby you all the way. You gotta learn how to use the UI to build and what the different factories require to produce. This game is like simcity but on mars. One thing though is I wish there was a way to skip the intro movie, cause I want to start a new game with out watching the intro. I like how your actions have consequences, I over build/overheated mars and cause to much flooding from melting ice.

developer_response.body:
Hello!

Thanks so much for taking the time to write us a review! We are glad you enjoyed the game even tho it's not what you usually play :3

We appreciate all the feedback and thanks again for supporting us.

Ad Astra!

PS: you can fast forward the intro using the right trigger.

reviews.review_description:

Hello developers so when you teraform mars what do you think probably making ot breathable Bet i think making it green when you run out off co2 what do you do you can use carbon extracter to release corbon into the air but that doesn't last ither let me introduce to you green house gases it will be easy to get but it takes longer for the leachen to spread and forest like carbon but a sub subsatute because I am tired of when I run out of carbon I don't get to see forest grow or even animals in fact I never saw a single tree or a animal in my game and I played three times but nothing and also the water stops spreading at around 2000 meters I know this game just released but you can't change the controls to oculus and then slap on the price and say pay up you have to put effort in it and I don't mean to offend you guys but I have faith and I will help with reporting bugs and giving ideas but please just keep putting effort and also I know that this game is realistic but when the grass spread does it also consume carbon or just the green house please let me know
Hello I am back could you please add trees and bird I thought that green mars was that tipe of 2.00 update
I finally found why I lose so much carbon during stage 4 you see that the aqua dome bacteria can keep spreading so my idea is that you can add barriers  and it will give you a percent on how much carbon it consumes and you much oxigen it makes and a nother idea I have why don't you add water domes that make carbon from oxigen and it will have the same features as the regular water dome and barriers it's all about balance so please consider my idea and make it free so everyone can make the big red planet there home.
Hello in the tech tree can you add carbon asteroid aerobreking please
And could you please post your updates on the oculus store please

developer_response.body:
Hello!

Thanks so much for taking the time to provide us with feedback, we are always happy to receive user reviews :3

Regarding the bugs, we'd like to ask you to fill reports for each one at this link https://rawfury.atlassian.net/servicedesk/customer/portal/1/group/1/create/18 this way we'll be able to track them better and look into the issues you mentioned.

And yes, the plants not only generate o2 but they consmue co2 as well.

Ad Astra!

reviews.review_title: Watch the green appear in real time
reviews.review_description:
If you’re in to base building simulation games, then you’re in for a treat. I’ve played about an hour, and it’s so good, and I can already tell it’s story rich. You should read one of the more in depth reviews too. I highly recommend this game.

Side note: zooming in and out is rlly cool

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for updating the review!

We are thrilled that you enjoyed the game.

Ad Astra!

reviews.review_description:
In the RTS-deprived Quest 2 ecosystem this is 100% essential if you dig the genre. Brilliant weaving of a great story (from one individual’s perspective, tough to do in this genre) into the macro of the game, which has you controlling a massive, but not overwhelming, system. And sandbox mode is also enjoyable. 

- MYSHIFTKEYISBROKE, Imperial Scrolls of Honor Podcast dude

developer_response.body:
Hey!

Thank you so, so much for all the praise :3 best of luck with the podcast!

Ad Astra!

reviews.review_description: Unable to select Drone Stack when attacked.
If you’re into Surviving Mars you will love this game!  I wish the music was as good though but that’s just a little thing.  Already spent 4 hours playing this tethered.  Lots of game play for sure!

developer_response.body:
Hello!

Thanks for playing our game and taking the time to give us a review! We really appreciate it :3

Ad Astra!

reviews.review_title: Per Aspera VR  the best city building VR
reviews.review_description:
I used to play city planning games, like Sim City (and most of the various versions) on my Mac for years. I enjoyed them and the level of detail and complexity. Per Aspera combines those types of details plus terraforming Mars for human inhabitants. At first I used the tutorials and “sandbox” to learn how to play. Now I’m playing the full game and it’s great.  I love the way you have to use limited resources to build the manufactured materials you will need to build more complex buildings for human habitation. I feel like I have a lot more to learn as the levels of play get more complex, but it’s great fun.
Q. For App Support: Did you change the name from Ad Astra? If so, why?
———————————————————
Update 10/27/23: 
I was confused at the references to AD Astra and somehow thought that was the name of the game until I tried to look it up and was surprised to see it as Per Aspera.  My mistake. Sorry

developer_response.body:
Hello!

Thanks a lot for taking the time to write us such a great review, we really appreciate it :3 and we are also thrilled that you are enjoying the game so much, it makes us extremely happy

Regarding your question, I'm not sure I fully understand, sorry. The game was always called Per Aspera, since the beginning. We only use Ad Astra as a sign off and sometimes, as a toast

Ad Astra!

reviews.review_description: This game/experience is the closest I've come to a proper rts game here. It's fun, and the animations are pretty awesome too.

I've never played this genre of game before, to be honest I've never even considered it. But, there was something about the notion of terraforming Mars that piqued my interest so I took the chance. 

I'm so glad I did!! I'm now at stage 4 of the process and I'd say that the game has the perfect blend of letting you get on with it and interrupting you with all sorts of drama and problems.

The only thing I'm not looking forward to is the game ending... maybe we'll get to terraform another planet in the next game by these devs??!... here's hoping.

developer_response.body:
Hi there!

Thanks so much for taking a chance on our game, we also took a chance by porting it to VR and we are extremely happy when we receive kind words like yours :3

Right now we haven't any announcements but keep an eye out in the future!

Ad Astra!

reviews.review_description:
Updated to five stars; I’m glad to see the opening credits can be bypassed now. 

The game itself is a lot of fun. But getting into the game takes a lot of time both in loading screens and, more importantly, having to watch (sloooooow) credits at the beginning of the campaign mode every new campaign. I’d give this 5 stars if not for how long it takes to actually start playing.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the update!

We are thrilled that you enjoyed the new FF during the intro, we worked really hard on it :3

Ad Astra!
